title: festuca ovina var. pallens
intro: Festuca ovina var. pallens, also known as Sheep's Fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a perennial plant that is native to Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory system. Additionally, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, and is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and prevent infections.
